title: Europe's Crypto Firms Face Squeeze as MiCA Transition Period End Looms
summary: As the MiCA grace period closes, only a fraction of registered firms hold full licenses, setting up the prospect of a wave of consolidation.
body:
The transition period for the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) in Europe is drawing to a close, and the results are clear: only a small portion of registered crypto firms have secured full licenses. This development sets the stage for a wave of consolidation in the industry as companies scramble to comply with the new regulatory framework.

According to reports, fewer than 20% of the registered crypto firms have obtained the necessary licenses to continue operating under MiCA. This leaves many companies at risk of being forced to shut down or merge with larger, licensed entities. The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) has stated that it will closely monitor the situation to ensure compliance and protect consumers.

For everyday crypto users, this consolidation could mean fewer choices in terms of exchanges and services. However, it may also lead to a more stable and secure crypto market, as only licensed firms will be able to operate legally. Users should be vigilant about the status of their preferred platforms and consider moving their assets to licensed providers if necessary.

Moving forward, crypto firms that have not yet obtained full licenses will need to act quickly to avoid being shut down. Users should watch for announcements from their current service providers regarding their licensing status and any potential changes in service. Staying informed and proactive will be key in navigating this shifting landscape. Read more → https://decrypt.co/371561/europes-crypto-firms-face-squeeze-mica-transition-period-end-looms
